vocab.meaning: tay nghề thủ công vô song/không đối thủ
vocab.example: I gravititate towards premium brands because they often offer unrivaled craftsmanship that local mass-produced shoes lack.

vocab.meaning: biểu tượng của địa vị/đẳng cấp
vocab.example: For many youngsters, owning a pair of limited-edition sneakers is less about comfort and more about having a status symbol.

vocab.meaning: sự bền bỉ/tuổi thọ lâu dài
vocab.example: While the upfront cost is higher, the longevity of designer footwear usually makes it a more sustainable choice in the long run.

vocab.meaning: sự hỗ trợ về công thái học (tốt cho cấu trúc cơ thể)
vocab.example: Famous athletic brands invest millions in R&D to provide superior ergonomic support for professional runners.

vocab.meaning: việc tiêu xài phô trương (mua đồ hiệu để khoe khoang)
vocab.example: I try to avoid conspicuous consumption; I only buy expensive brands if the quality truly justifies the exorbitant price tag.

vocab.meaning: thiết kế tiên phong/tối tân nhất
vocab.example: Major fashion houses are always at the forefront of the industry, consistently releasing cutting-edge designs that set global trends.

vocab.meaning: sự trung thành với thương hiệu
vocab.example: My brand loyalty stems from years of positive experiences with their sizing and durability.

vocab.meaning: chất lượng dưới mức tiêu chuẩn
vocab.example: I’ve had enough of subpar quality from off-brand shoes that fall apart after just a few months of wear.

vocab.meaning: quy trình sản xuất có đạo đức
vocab.example: Nowadays, I tend to support famous brands that are transparent about their commitment to ethical manufacturing and fair wages.
Highly coveted brands like certain Italian labels maintain an impressive resale value, even after they've been worn.
